prestazioni: Engine swap to a Porsche 912/8 (Type 616/36) 2.0L flat-four with Mahle forged pistons (87.5mm bore), Carrillo connecting rods, and a custom Schrick 284° camshaft for high-RPM performance, paired with a Weber 48 IDA carburetor setup and a custom stainless steel exhaust system by Fabspeed with 4-2-1 headers and a straight-pipe center section for aggressive tone and flow

Full suspension overhaul with Bilstein B16 coilovers (custom valved for the 356 Speedster’s lightweight chassis), Tarett Engineering spherical bearing control arms, and a custom 15mm rear torsion bar upgrade for improved cornering stiffness, complemented by a 22mm front sway bar from Elephant Racing

Lightweight flywheel from Fidanza (10.5 lbs) paired with a Sachs Performance twin-disc clutch (ceramic-metallic friction material) and a Quaife ATB differential (80% lockup) for improved power delivery and traction under hard acceleration
estetica: Custom hand-formed aluminum widebody kit by RWB (Rauh-Welt Begriff) adapted for the 356 Speedster, featuring riveted fender flares, a lowered roofline, and integrated side skirts with functional air vents for brake cooling

Full leather interior reupholstery by Recaro with custom Speedster-specific SRD bucket seats (carbon fiber shells, Alcantara centers, and red stitching), a MOMO Prototipo steering wheel (380mm diameter, suede grip), and a custom aluminum shift knob by BBI Offroad with a Porsche crest engraved in red

Custom paint scheme by Strosek Design: a two-tone matte black (RAL 9005) and signal red (RAL 3001) with hand-painted gold pinstriping, complemented by Hella 160 headlights (clear lenses, yellow bulbs) and a custom rear ducktail spoiler fabricated from carbon fiber by 9ff
